evidence_tiers.E0: Infrastructure, synthetic evidence, fixtures, or operational dry run; no edge claim.
evidence_tiers.E1: Real-data diagnostic evidence that is under-sampled, underpowered, or gate-blocked.
evidence_tiers.E2: Validation-grade evidence passing preregistered statistics, regimes, robustness, costs, and adversarial review.
evidence_tiers.E3: E2 plus operational validation, account feasibility, and a separate launch checklist.

required: DSR or an explicit blocker when search occurred

hypotheses.statement: At least one globally diversified US-listed fractional-ETF trend implementation is honestly sizeable at USD 1,000 and USD 2,000 after broker constraints and costs, and the same study can identify minimum viable capital for 4-, 8-, and 12-market micro-futures variants.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: A statistical edge has no operational value when share or contract granularity, margin, cash buffers, costs, or concentration prevent risk-targeted breadth.
testable_predictions: Fractional ETFs permit materially finer risk allocation than futures at current capital.
Eight to twelve global economic sleeves are feasible at current capital only when fractional execution and minimum-trade rules permit risk-balanced allocation.
Minimum viable micro-futures capital rises with breadth, cash buffer, and volatility stress.
validation_criteria: Preregister target risk, concentration, cash buffer, minimum trade, annualized cost, and broker-capability thresholds before measurement.
Classify USD 1,000 and USD 2,000 separately without a strategy return backtest.
Report micro-futures minimum capital for 4, 8, and 12 markets using one-contract granularity and stressed margin.
falsification_criteria: Kill the current-capital claim if no broker-realistic ETF configuration meets all locked breadth, cash-buffer, concentration, minimum-trade, and annualized-cost thresholds at either USD 1,000 or USD 2,000.
A single broker or API capability failure is a scope constraint unless every preregistered implementation path fails.
required_data: ETF price, volatility, liquidity, inception, currency, and fractional eligibility data.
Webull Thailand fees, fractional rules, API capability, and sandbox/account eligibility.
IBKR account-reported permissions, fees, and API capability.
Micro and full-size futures multipliers, tick values, initial/maintenance margins, and stressed margin ranges.

decision_log.notes: Owner capital is USD 1,000-2,000; Webull Thailand is the ETF candidate and IBKR is the micro-futures reference.

Eight- and ten-sleeve ETF economic sizing passes at USD 1,000 and USD 2,000, while twelve sleeves breach the locked 25% weight cap. No broker path is fully verified because exact fractional eligibility, Thailand OpenAPI capability, funding FX, or account permissions remain unknown. Micro-futures minimum capital is USD 40,600, 54,200, and 95,400 for 4, 8, and 12 markets; no edge or deployment claim is made.

The owner's Webull Thailand production application passed authentication, account-list, balance, and positions read-only calls. All ten locked ETF candidates returned status OC and fractionable true. L-0 remains scope-restricted because minimum order, funding FX, execution quality, and realized costs were not tested; no preview, order, paper trade, edge, E2, validation unlock, or real-money action is authorized.

Gate l_0_webull_th_fractional_preview_probe_v1 hash-locks the fail-closed runner, exact Thailand UAT VTI quantity grid, request guard, report validator, and three synthetic fixtures. B4.10 made no broker, provider, authentication, preview, order, or validation request. A separate owner-approved B4.11 activation gate is required before credential access or execution; L-0 remains scope-restricted E0.

The owner explicitly approved one bounded Webull Thailand UAT preview execution. Gate l_0_webull_th_fractional_preview_activation_v1 is hash-locked before credential or network access, but all three required WEBULL_UAT variables are absent. No API request occurred; L-0 remains scope-restricted E0.

The one authorized run used the official shared UAT credential row in process memory only. Token create plus two token checks consumed the locked three-request authentication budget; the guard blocked the fourth authentication attempt. No preview, order, production, provider, or validation request occurred. The result establishes no fractional minimum and L-0 remains scope-restricted E0.

Gate l_0_webull_th_fractional_preview_activation_v2 preserves the B4.10/B4.11 artifacts and funds one token create plus at most seven token checks over a fixed 30-second SDK polling window. The VTI quantity grid and all production, order, privacy, provider, validation, claim, and zero-spend boundaries remain unchanged. No B4.12 network request has occurred at the gate stage; L-0 remains scope-restricted E0.

After the v2 gate commit and CI passed, the one authorized run made one token-create and seven token-check requests. Authentication remained non-NORMAL at 30 seconds, so no preview, order, production, provider, or validation request occurred. The result does not establish whether any VTI quantity is accepted and L-0 remains scope-restricted E0.

hypotheses.statement: A 60-day directional-count baseline has positive convex payoff after implementable costs on the selected global research universe.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Slow information diffusion followed by herding can produce continuation, while the system accepts frequent small losses to retain infrequent large trends.
testable_predictions: Net performance is driven by right-tail trend episodes rather than hit rate.
Whipsaw and mean-reverting regimes are the primary loss zone.
The result survives removal of the best market and best trend episode.
validation_criteria: Positive implementable net return and preregistered convexity/right-tail criteria.
PSR passes against zero and a matched implementable benchmark; DSR passes or is blocked explicitly if search occurred.
Actual independent-bet-equivalent count meets MinTRL_validate and required regime coverage or explicit scope restrictions.
falsification_criteria: After MinTRL_falsify, implementable net return is non-positive and the preregistered right-tail/convexity condition fails across at least two independent trend-regime buckets.
A written mechanism autopsy identifies why continuation or its system preconditions failed.
required_data: Survivorship-clean global ETF and/or contract histories with inception/delisting treatment.
Futures roll-aware histories if the futures research branch is used.
Implementable spread, commission, slippage, currency, and roll costs.

mintrl_falsify.observation_unit: autocorrelation-adjusted portfolio returns and independent-bet equivalents

decision_log.notes: No empirical evidence exists at founding.

Gate l_1_baseline_v1 locks the 60-day baseline and sequential falsification-before-validation protocol. This is a research specification only; no L-1 market evidence, edge claim, or execution result exists.

E1 falsification-window evidence funds MinTRL_falsify but does not meet the full two-regime falsification rule. Net performance, payoff shape, concentration, data provenance, cash, historical fees, and broker feasibility remain blocked. Validation stays sealed.

E1 remediation resolves the zero-cash assumption and decision-bounds historical fee uncertainty without turning primary net CAGR positive. Six of eight corporate-action reconciliations pass the locked daily tolerance; point-in-time revision provenance remains unavailable. Webull Thailand public material verifies manual fractional trading generally but not candidate-ticker eligibility or fractional OpenAPI. Validation remains sealed and no edge or deployment claim is made.

The calendar-only sealed window has 2,637 NYSE sessions and projects to 20,376.00 joint independent-bet equivalents under the locked pre-2016 actual-dependence recalculation, above the 8,673 binding MinTRL. The founding planning sensitivity projects only 7,603.64. No validation market observations were accessed; corporate-action provenance and a separate owner-approved unlock gate remain blockers. Databento metadata is authenticated but begins in 2018 or later for relevant US-equity datasets and exposes no dedicated corporate-actions history.

The locked free acquisition completed 16/16 corporate-action payloads and 772 rows with zero paid spend. Eleven of sixteen pre-2016 symbol-endpoint pairs reconcile exactly; five dividend pairs do not. Alpha Vantage exposes a current snapshot rather than a point-in-time revision archive. Validation returns remain sealed, and no E2, edge, or deployment claim is permitted.

The owner accepts the unresolved historical corporate-action difference as an explicit E1 limitation and pauses new or paid source search. Validation remains sealed. Any future comparison requires a separate E0 operational dry-run preregistration with edge_claim none, materiality thresholds locked before observation, and no historical-correctness or E2 interpretation.

Gate l_1_prospective_shadow_accounting_v1 locks three streams, the fixed L-0 ten-sleeve accounting portfolio, account-scaled materiality thresholds, no netting, at least 180 days plus three matched events across two symbols, and a 365-day stop. No broker or provider call, event observation, activation, paper order, validation access, edge, E2, historical-correctness, deployment, or real-money claim is authorized.

Gate l_1_shadow_accounting_activation_v1 blocks activation because public Webull Thailand evidence does not establish a dedicated owner-controlled test ledger, account-level corporate-action cash/unit paths, or the minimum fractional-share quantum. Runtime network and order allowlists are empty, request caps are zero, no forward marker exists, and no broker/provider call or prospective observation occurred. Written Webull Thailand confirmation plus a new owner-approved superseding gate is required. Validation remains sealed and L-1 remains scope-restricted E1.

Gate l_1_shadow_accounting_activation_v2 supersedes v1. The owner accepts the published Webull Thailand API as the current scope, so the absent account-level corporate-action ledger is treated as unavailable rather than awaiting written confirmation. The B4.7 three-stream dry run closes without observation; Alpha Vantage and Lily Yahoo accounting remain two limited non-ground-truth research streams. A representative eight-request VTI UAT preview grid is preregistered but not executed. Validation remains sealed and L-1 remains scope-restricted E1.

The L-0 fractional-preview machinery is hash-locked with an E0 ceiling but was not executed. It adds no broker, corporate-action, return, edge, or validation evidence to L-1; the validation window remains sealed and L-1 remains scope-restricted E1.

Owner approval and the activation gate authorize only the bounded L-0 UAT preview. Missing UAT variables prevent execution, and no broker or validation observation occurred. L-1 remains scope-restricted E1 with validation sealed.

The guarded UAT run stopped after three authentication requests and before any preview. It adds no broker-ledger, corporate-action, return, edge, or validation evidence to L-1. Validation remains sealed and L-1 remains scope-restricted E1.

The B4.12 superseding gate changes only the bounded L-0 shared-UAT authentication budget and preserves the validation seal. Before execution it adds no broker-ledger, corporate-action, return, edge, or validation evidence to L-1, which remains scope-restricted E1.

The single B4.12 shared-UAT run exhausted its fixed 30-second authentication window before preview. It adds no broker-ledger, corporate-action, price, return, edge, or validation evidence to L-1. Validation remains sealed and L-1 remains scope-restricted E1.

hypotheses.statement: A 32/64/126/252 multi-lookback t-stat signal improves the Sharpe of paired daily net active returns versus the matched-horizon directional-count comparator; the 60-day L-1 baseline is secondary reference only.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Combining horizons may reduce dependence on one arbitrary lookback while retaining trend exposure.
testable_predictions: Improvement survives matched universe, timing, sizing, and cost assumptions.
Improvement is not explained solely by greater leverage or a different effective horizon.
A locked risk-adjusted or turnover/cost component improves outside the search sample.
validation_criteria: Lock one utility rule before execution and compare paired dates with identical portfolio plumbing.
Log every trial and require DSR or untouched revalidation.
Meet MinTRL_validate using autocorrelation-adjusted effective observations.
falsification_criteria: After MinTRL_falsify, the paired active-return utility is below its locked margin with a primary-margin PSR at most 0.05.
A mechanism autopsy explains whether horizon aggregation added noise, lag, or cost.
required_data: The exact L-1 universe, dates, normalized inputs, and costs.
A complete candidate search log and untouched evaluation block.

decision_log.notes: Candidate remains a design input, not a preferred result.

Gate l_2_multi_lookback_tstat_v1 locks the matched-protocol 32/64/126/252 t-stat candidate, paired utility, DSR trial log, dual MinTRL, and sealed validation boundary. No L-2 market result, edge claim, broker/provider action, or validation access exists.

Gate l_2_multi_lookback_tstat_v2 supersedes v1 without mutation. It makes the matched-horizon directional-count signal the primary comparator, keeps L-1 60-day as secondary reference, and locks per-period paired-active-return MinTRL and DSR inputs. No L-2 market result or validation access exists.

hypotheses.statement: Inverse-volatility sizing reduces portfolio risk concentration relative to equal notional without unacceptable turnover, leverage, or cost.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Equal notional allows volatile markets to dominate risk; volatility scaling should distribute risk more evenly.
testable_predictions: Maximum asset and sleeve risk contributions fall.
Concentration reduction survives volatility regimes and estimator perturbations.
The benefit is not erased by resizing turnover or leverage.
validation_criteria: Lock ex-ante and realized concentration metrics, estimator windows, leverage/cash limits, turnover, and regimes.
Meet the paired effective-sample validation gate.
falsification_criteria: After MinTRL_falsify, inverse-volatility sizing does not reduce locked concentration metrics in most required regimes or its benefit is fully offset by locked cost/leverage limits.
A mechanism autopsy explains estimator, correlation, or implementation failure.
required_data: Matched asset returns, volatility estimates, positions, costs, and realized risk contributions.

mintrl_falsify.observation_unit: paired concentration observations with serial-dependence adjustment

decision_log.notes: Sizing architecture hypothesis; excluded from the mechanism family-stop count.

hypotheses.statement: Adding economically distinct countries, asset sleeves, and markets reduces dependency on any single-market trend after costs.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Large trends are rare and synchronized markets are not independent bets; genuine breadth should expand the opportunity set.
testable_predictions: Top-market and top-sleeve PnL and risk shares fall as genuine breadth increases.
Drawdown dependency and trend-state concentration decline.
Benefits survive removal of the best market and the best trend episode.
validation_criteria: Compare nested survivorship-clean universes on matched dates and costs.
Report return correlation, trend-state correlation, concentration, and independent-bet measures.
Meet MinTRL_validate or record a scope restriction.
falsification_criteria: After MinTRL_falsify, broader universes fail to reduce locked dependency metrics or the apparent benefit disappears after costs and best-market removal across required regimes.
A mechanism autopsy explains synchronization, redundant exposure, or cost failure.
required_data: Point-in-time universe membership, inception/delisting records, country and asset-sleeve labels, costs, and trend-state histories.

mintrl_falsify.observation_unit: nested-universe dependency differences with serial and cross-sectional dependence adjustment

decision_log.notes: Global exposure uses US-listed ETFs for fractional execution but must span countries and asset classes.
